programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil travels to Tunstall in Kent, to the country cottage of Sarah Sewells and Andrew Morris. The couple bought the house two years ago, but with marriage on the horizon, they'd love to move to a bigger family house.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan is worried about crime, and decides that a burglar alarm is the answer.

programme.title: The Truman Show
programme.desc: Truman doesn't realise that his quaint hometown is a giant studio set run by a visionary director, that folks living and working there are Hollywood actors, and that even his incessantly bubbly wife is a contract player.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Carter gains some perspective when he joins Luka in the Congo, where the two doctors find their surroundings much more different than what they left behind in Chicago, as Luka and Carter must overcome army fighting to treat patients.

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Comedy legend Denise Scott joins Alice in the kitchen. Before she arrives, Alice whips up an '80s classic - the scotch egg. Denise pops in with a basket full of vegetables asking Alice to help her fall back in love with them.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Beating the drum for general ignorance are Mark Gatiss, Sean Lock, Linda Smith and Alan Davies, who tackle questions about music and sound. Stephen Fry conducts proceedings.

programme.title: Would I Lie To You?
programme.desc: Sorting the facts from the fiction alongside host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell are guests Bob Mortimer, Samantha Morton, Miles Jupp and Sarah Hadland.

programme.title: Mythbusters
programme.desc: Deadliest Catch captains Johnathan and Junior join the MythBusters to test if a crab pot will drag you overboard to the bottom if your foot is caught in a coil, if it's better to work a 30-hour grind with no sleep, and if crab pots truly are indestructible.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil comes to the rescue of grandmother Elaine Care, whose four bedroom semi in Folkestone has been on the market for a whopping two years.

programme.title: Grand Designs UK
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Ed Versluys and Vicky Anderson who sold up and abandoned their city lives and bought an ugly, derelict 1940s concrete cowshed with amazing views over the Somerset countryside.

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Suzanne Dowling, Wally De Backer, Denise Scott & Scott Edgar
programme.desc: Joining Adam Hills tonight are Suzanne Dowling, Wally De Backer, Denise Scott and Scott Edgar.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig continues airing questions on the theme of Quests, this time inviting Susan Calman, Joe Lycett and Holly Walsh to join regular panellist Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie To You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show.

programme.title: Attenborough's Global Adventure
programme.desc: In his mid-eighties, many thought David Attenborough had retired. Instead, he launched a new era of filmmaking, revisiting iconic locations and using advanced technologies to tell stories about the natural world.

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: Renowned interviewer, Leigh Sales mentors autistic journalism students in The Assembly. In this episode, celebrated actor and producer Claudia Karvan shares insights into her work and relationships.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: When Carter makes his return from Africa, he soon finds himself facing a cold reception from Abby, but Carter is soon called back to the Congo, after he is informed of a friend's death; Pratt flirts with a new med student: Romano returns to work.

programme.title: Offsiders
programme.desc: For 20 years, Offsiders has been here to unpack the week in sport. Abbey Gelmi and the panel discuss the latest AFL, NRL, Women's Rugby League State of Origin, Men's and Women's A-League Finals, Super Rugby and Super Netball.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan starts to worry about rising crime in the area and thinks a burglar alarm is the answer. Unfortunately, she hasn't taken into account the rest of the family's ineptitude. Meanwhile, Nick decides to become a stripper.

programme.title: Rage New Music
programme.desc: Discover the wildest new music videos on rage. Essential grooves and boundary-pushing visuals from Australia and beyond.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Quiz show in which the aim is to be interesting. Stephen Fry meditates on matters medieval and macabre with Julia Zemiro, Matt Lucas and David Mitchell.

programme.title: The Messenger
programme.desc: Ed finally ticks off all the messages on the Ace of Diamonds but then another ace appears and Ed realises this is only the beginning.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Beating the drum for general ignorance are Mark Gatiss, Sean Lock, Linda Smith and Alan Davies, who tackle questions about music and sound. Stephen Fry conducts proceedings.

programme.title: ABC News Update (Melbourne)
programme.desc: The headlines from ABC News, updating you on the day's top stories from across Australia and around the world.

programme.title: Grand Designs UK
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Ed Versluys and Vicky Anderson who sold up and abandoned their city lives and bought an ugly, derelict 1940s concrete cowshed with amazing views over the Somerset countryside.

programme.title: Star Wars: Young Jedi Adventures
programme.desc: Nash and the young Jedi pursue stolen jellyfruit.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series
programme.desc: Mavis accidentally loses a magic wand and it is found by the worst person possible. And so now, all of Hotel Transylvania is trapped inside Kitty Cartwright's snow globe!

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series
programme.desc: Mavis Dracula finally got a word in the Vamptionary, the essential tome of all words relating to vampires. But did she come up with the word or did some other monster?

programme.title: The InBESTigators
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Misplaced Mug
programme.desc: Mr. McGillick is upset after he loses his precious 'World's Best Principal' mug, so Ezra makes it his personal mission to get it back.

programme.title: The InBESTigators
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Sad Little Sister
programme.desc: Poppy loses her school hat and is forced to sit in the shelter shed, where Maudie discovers there may be more to the missing hat.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales Of Ladybug And Cat Noir
programme.desc: Humiliated by Kim, Otis the zookeeper gets akumatized into a black panther. Now Animan, he causes mayhem in town. If they want to stop him, Ladybug and Cat Noir will have to chase him across the Parisian jungle!

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales Of Ladybug And Cat Noir
programme.desc: After being humiliated while performing a hypnotist act, Simon gets akumatized into Simon Says and is able to get anyone to do his bidding.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: It's Crime Season, and Robin continues his winning streak.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Bitzer thinks the future has arrived when he takes delivery of his new high tech farmyard control module but when the gadget malfunctions, Bitzer is left wishing for his old whistle.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: Dancer Maddie Ziegler needs help with a haunted studio.

programme.title: We Baby Bears
programme.desc: The Bears find a mysterious crayon that has the power to make anything they draw become REAL.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: An alarmingly familiar cat burglar terrorizes Townsville.

programme.title: Open Season: Call Of Nature
programme.desc: Boog feels sorry that Ian hasn't received love tokens during the annual Springtime Sweethearts event, so he takes pity on him and donates one anonymously so that Ian doesn't feel left out. Big Mistake.

programme.title: Summer Camp Island
programme.desc: The campers spend a rainy day at the library.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Watterson family faces Mom's wrath in a supermarket.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Justice League's Next Top Talent Idol Star: Second Greatest Team Edition: Part 2
programme.desc: The competition heats up and the Titans must find a way to beat the Hive Five.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg's real strength comes not from his robot parts, but from his favorite song.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales Of Ladybug And Cat Noir
programme.desc: While visiting the Louvre museum, Marinette discovers the secrets of the kwagatama that Tikki gave her on her birthday.

programme.title: Float On
programme.desc: Unikitty plans a parade to celebrate being unique - with everyone designing their own personalized floats! But Master Frown plans to ruin the parade with a super depressing float!

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.sub-title: Eyes to the Skies Once Again: Part Two
programme.desc: After a year apart, Roy visits Liko at Indigo Academy to ask for her help.

programme.title: Gg: Spawn Squad
programme.desc: Sophie and Gem explore the many sides of Peach in Princess Peach Showtime! Watch them sneak, fight and yeehaw their way through wild and wonderful levels before taking on the violet villain herself - Madame Grape!

programme.title: Dance With Tom
programme.desc: Tom teaches a Nyunga dance. Nyunga is the Yugambeh word for Sun, which is the source of our energy! Join Tom for a Nyunga dance to warm your body and help you start your day!

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.sub-title: You're Five Ones and I'm Five Tens
programme.desc: Superstar Fifty sings a song that shows Five the pattern that makes them similar in so many ways and how, If you can count to five, you can count to fifty!

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Agents recall how a beloved gadget helped them.

programme.title: Understanding Numbers
programme.desc: Learn how to accurately name ordinal numbers from first to fifth. Ordinal numbers help us describe the position of people or objects in a sequence.

programme.title: Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Counting in multiples of 2, 5, and 10, calculating answers using objects, language of multiplication.

programme.title: Wow That's Amazing
programme.desc: In this episode, find out how to blow tunes on balloons, discover a way to totally trick your senses, learn not to believe everything you see in the mirror, and take a look at the universe using a giant telescope!

programme.title: Scigirls
programme.desc: Virginia SciGirls Emma, Lauren and Madison have the ultimate \.

programme.title: Dr Karl's How Things Work
programme.desc: Guided by a pie-tastic team in Perth, Dr Karl embarks on a culinary odyssey to uncover how Australia's iconic meat pies are made.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil comes to the rescue of grandmother Elaine Care, whose four bedroom semi in Folkestone has been on the market for a whopping two years.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When a Chinese businessman in tour guide Susan's party suddenly drops dead in Madame Tussaud's Susan begins to worry about Ben's mortality - as indeed does he after a chat to the vicar. This gives Nick the opportunity to bring would-be girlfriend and insurance salesperson Emily to the house to sell Ben a policy. Ben agrees, in the hopes that it will encourage Nick to move out but Susan has already got him a policy.

programme.title: Shetland
programme.desc: The explosion prompts Perez to issue a warrant for Connor's arrest, to the outrage of Rachel Cairns. The team speak to those who knew him in search of extremist beliefs. Jamie, Murray and Lloyd reject any notion that Connor is capable of violence, but a despairing Danny admits environmental issues made his son angry. Meanwhile, Sandy searches for William Rodgers' reason for being in Shetland.

programme.title: Attenborough's Global Adventure
programme.desc: In his mid-eighties, many thought David Attenborough had retired. Instead, he launched a new era of filmmaking, revisiting iconic locations and using advanced technologies to tell stories about the natural world.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: When Carter makes his return from Africa, he soon finds himself facing a cold reception from Abby, but Carter is soon called back to the Congo, after he is informed of a friend's death; Pratt flirts with a new med student: Romano returns to work.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry quizzes Alan Davies, Jo Brand, Phill Jupitus and Clive Anderson in the mind-boggling panel game.

programme.title: Would I Lie To You?
programme.sub-title: Warwick Davis, Hugh Dennis, Katherine Ryan & John Simpson
programme.desc: The award-winning comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, returns with team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell. This week’s guests include actor Warwick Davis, comedian Hugh Dennis, stand-up star Katherine Ryan, and journalist John Simpson, all ready to deliver sharp wit, clever bluffs, and hilarious stories in the battle of truths and lies.

programme.title: Mythbusters
programme.desc: MythBusters' 10th anniversary kicks off with a blast from the past, the myth that began the busting: JATO Rocket Car. Back with more rocket power, more gadgets, and a huge rolling bunker called The Beast, will the team bust this myth once and for all?

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil is tasked with helping Victoria and Michael shift the grand Victorian house they've called home for the past 30 years. The property is wearing them out but the trouble is, the interior is as tired as they are.

programme.title: Grand Designs UK
programme.sub-title: South Downs: The Rusty Metal House
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Stephen Yeoman and Anita Findlay who want to build a cutting edge, post-industrial house. Their plot is one of the most prominent in the South Downs - so everyone will be watching.

programme.title: Claire Hooper's House Of Games
programme.desc: Staying over at Claire Hooper's House of Games this week, we’ve got actor Gyton Grantley, netballer Bianca Chatfield, and comedians Jenny Tian and Nazeem Hussain tackling puzzles for a shot at a toilet seat, and a trophy.

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Frank Ifield, Brian Cadd, Mikey Robins, Cal Wilson
programme.desc: Joining Adam Hills are Frank Ifield, Brian Cadd, Mikey Robins, Cal Wilson, and Collard Greens & Gravy in this ARIA special episode.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry looks at the multi-sized world of the little and the large. With Richard Osman, Lucy Porter, Phill Jupitus and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie To You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host's chair, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell as the lightning-quick team captains. Sorting the fact from the fiction this week are guests Angellica Bell, Bob Mortimer, Suggs and Holly Willoughby.

programme.title: The Outlaws
programme.desc: A few months on, the outlaws still have time to serve on their sentences. But they discover the criminal underworld isn't done with them yet. Thrown out by her parents, Rani pitches up at Christian's.

programme.title: Elementary
programme.desc: Holmes returns to New York with a new apprentice and a renewed interest in working with the NYPD after being fired by London's MI6. Unfortunately, Joan, the NYPD's new go-to PI, must give her approval first.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Going into harm's way, Carter returns to the Congo to search for Luka's body despite the chaos and danger of a civil war. Taken prisoner by rebel troops, Luka's fate is revealed from the time Carter left 22 days earlier.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When a Chinese businessman in tour guide Susan's party suddenly drops dead in Madame Tussaud's Susan begins to worry about Ben's mortality - as indeed does he after a chat to the vicar. This gives Nick the opportunity to bring would-be girlfriend and insurance salesperson Emily to the house to sell Ben a policy. Ben agrees, in the hopes that it will encourage Nick to move out but Susan has already got him a policy.

programme.title: rage Guest Programmer: Melanie C
programme.desc: From Duran Duran, TLC, The Prodigy, Madonna and more, join former Spice Girl Melanie C as hand-picks her favourite music videos on rage.

programme.title: Mother's Day
programme.desc: From Garry Marshall; the director of Pretty Woman and Valentine's Day; comes Mother's Day; an all-star comedy that celebrates parenthood and family. Jennifer Aniston; Julia Roberts; Kate Hudson and Jason Sudeikis lead an incredible cast in a film that intersects the lives of a group of people maneuvering their way through a crazy week of difficult relationships and family dysfunction in the wee...

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts a nosey, noisy episode of QI with Aisling Bea, Corey Taylor, Ross Noble and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie To You?
programme.sub-title: Julie Hesmondhalgh, Chris McCausland, Yinka Bokinni & Harriet Kemsley
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the award-winning comedy panel show. Team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell are joined by guest panellists Julie Hesmondhalgh, Chris McCausland, Yinka Bokinni and Harriet Kemsley.

programme.title: ABC News Update
programme.desc: The headlines from ABC News, updating you on the day's top stories from across Australia and around the world.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Joining QI master Stephen Fry are Clive Anderson, Jo Brand, Phill Jupitus and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Star Wars: Young Jedi Adventures
programme.desc: The younglings discover a bird-like creature.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series
programme.desc: Aunt Lydia is looking for a stale, new sound to replace the Hotel's elevator music. And Mavis has discovered just the thing: a sleep-yodelling Donald Cartwright.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series
programme.desc: Mavis and the gang discover Quasimodo's secret recipe for dirt flambe which leads to uncovering Quasimodo's even bigger secret - that he's been hiding out for 50 years!

programme.title: The InBESTigators
programme.sub-title: The Case Of The Disoriented Drone
programme.desc: After a little girl's drone is blown away, the Inbestigators must prove that a boy in a neighbouring house has claimed it as his own.

programme.title: The InBESTigators
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Baffling Birthday
programme.desc: Ava wants to give Maudie her first proper birthday party, but Maudie gets sidetracked by a mystery.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales Of Ladybug And Cat Noir
programme.desc: Ladybug and Cat Noir face Vincent Asa. Now Pixelator, he wants to capture the image of his idol Jagged Stone for good!

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales Of Ladybug And Cat Noir
programme.desc: When an arrogant, young singer angers Jagged Stone, he is akumatized into a rock 'n' roll super villain.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin's streak is in jeopardy when his team is poached by Kid Flash.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: When one of the pigs attempts to make friends with Timmy, his cohort evict him as punishment for un-pig-like behaviour. The sheep take him into the barn and attempt to transform him into the epitome of porcine sophistication.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.sub-title: The Wedding Witch of Wainsly Hall!
programme.desc: Scooby and our gang of mystery solving teens have been invited by famous comedian Jeff Foxworthy to help him move an old mansion that he's just purchased.

programme.title: We Baby Bears
programme.desc: The Bears accidentally summon three mysterious witches and have to figure out how to get them back home.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: When Buttercup loses the Professor's latest invention, she'll have to beat The Gangreen Gang at their own game to get it back.

programme.title: Open Season: Call Of Nature
programme.desc: With his wagon bursting at the seams, Weenie is revealed to be something of an obsessive collector, so Giselle suggests that he clear out all of his old stuff by having a yard sale. But can Weenie let go of the past?

programme.title: Summer Camp Island
programme.desc: Oscar’s fib about his last baby tooth alerts the Molar Mole Fraud Squad, and Hedgehog jumps to his defense at a kooky mole trial.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin must apologise to everyone they know after getting a threatening message on Elmore Plus.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When he isn't invited to Beast Boy's birthday party, Robin throws his own party, with special guest Bob Uecker.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin convinces the Titans to go back to school with him so he can finally become the all-American class president-type he's dreamt of being. Control Freak informs the Titans that they are on a TV show he created and demands they earn him an award. Cyborg's real strength comes not from his robot parts, but from his favourite song. Robin tries to teach the Titans valuable life lessons by reading them fairy tales, but the others co-opt his stories and spin them into their own twisted yarns.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales Of Ladybug And Cat Noir
programme.desc: As Ladybug and Cat Noir face Glaciator again, our heroes don't know that they are about to make a chilling discovery!

programme.title: Tooth Trouble
programme.desc: Puppycorn loses his first tooth and Unikitty's excited for her little bro-- now he​ ​gets a visit from​ ​the​ ​legendary Tooth Fairy!​

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: Liko, Roy and Dot investigate a report of Pokémon affected by Laquium.

programme.title: Gg: Spawn Squad
programme.sub-title: Flying Into Kirby Air Riders with Zac!
programme.desc: Zac and Gem take a first look at Kirby Air Riders! Exploring new game mechanics, strange control schemes and epic new courses, this episode has everything you need to know about the new racer!

programme.title: Dance With Tom
programme.desc: Tom teaches a Mibunn dance. Mibunn is the Yugambeh word for Wedge Tail Eagle, which flies high and wide, watching over all the creatures of the land. Join Tom in a Mibunn dance to look over and protect the land!

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Thirty-Six and Forty-Five are exploring the Forest of Tens, on a quest for Ten. But what is the special something they need to find and bring to Ten?

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The agents bring someone with an odd problem back to headquarters.

programme.title: Our Animals
programme.desc: Koalas are a special type of marsupial that live high in the canopy of gum trees where they eat leaves and sleep for most of the day.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dr Xand gets in a spin to show you why you get dizzy, the Flying Doctors help a new baby in Australia and Dr Raph takes an icy dip when he meets a Brilliant Body who swims in the Antarctic.

programme.title: Physics House
programme.sub-title: Why Does My Toast Land Buttered Side Down?
programme.desc: Why is it that toast usually seems to land buttered-side down? Is it the weight of the butter? Or are there other forces at work? Join Yanick in the Physics House and get to the physics behind your ruined breakfast.

programme.title: btn
programme.desc: BTN is a high-energy, fun way for Upper Primary and Secondary students to learn about current issues and events in their world. Each program is built around major news stories of the week.

programme.title: The Slow Mo Guys' Big Adventures
programme.desc: What makes a cannon fire? How far can a cannonball go? The Slow Mo Guys go medieval.

programme.title: Elevator Pitch
programme.desc: Our universe has been expanding ever since it was created. But what's causing this, and will it continue forever? Rad Yeo challenges astrophysicist Professor Tara Murphy to tackle this cosmic question in this elevator ride.

programme.title: BtN Newsbreak
programme.desc: Find out what's going on in the world with a news bulletin designed for children, featuring stories that are relevant and easy to understand.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil is tasked with helping Victoria and Michael shift the grand Victorian house they've called home for the past 30 years. The property is wearing them out but the trouble is, the interior is as tired as they are.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Michael is about to go on his first date, but Ben's plans to give him 'the big talk' are doomed when Nick offers his own advice first. Meanwhile, Susan fails to understand the importance of Prada jeans in Janey's life.

programme.title: Shetland
programme.desc: Perez tries to get the measure of Lloyd. Evidence emerges of another bombmaker. The net closes in around Martin. Sandy uncovers Connor's interest in a piece of local history.

programme.title: Elementary
programme.desc: Holmes returns to New York with a new apprentice and a renewed interest in working with the NYPD after being fired by London's MI6. Unfortunately, Joan, the NYPD's new go-to PI, must give her approval first.
